title: lavatera triloba var. rotundata
intro: Lavatera triloba var. rotundata, also known as the tree mallow, is a perennial plant species in the mallow family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of tree mallow include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of tree mallow can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
